operations.notes:
Use this API to manually trigger retrieval of topology data collection.
This functionality is applicable to all root devices with dynamic topology setting enabled.
The topology data will be retrieved for the root device and all of its managed devices.
Use /securetrack/api/devices/bulk/tasks/{task_uid} API to track the progress of the retrieval.
After the task status is complete, run Fast Topology Sync to see the updated device topology on the map.

Use this API to delete SecureTrack devices in bulk.
This function is supported for all devices.
The input for API is the Management ID (mgmt_ID).
Use the mgmt_ID of a management device to delete it and its managed devices.
Use multiple mgmt_IDs to delete individual devices in bulk.

Use this API to add a single device or a list of devices to SecureTrack.
The following devices can be added: Check Point CMA R80, SMC R80, Smart 1 Cloud; Cisco ASA; Cisco Router; Cisco Nexus; Cisco XR; Cisco Meraki; Palo Alto Panorama NG; Fortinet FortiManager; Microsoft Azure; Amazon AWS; Arista CVP and EOS
The API body should include a JSON file with the mandatory parameters for the operation.
String parameters contain only lower and upper case letters, digits, space, dot, backward slash, forward slash, underscore or one of the following signs: #@:;=!^().
Currently supported types: CMA, SMC, Smart 1 Cloud, ASA, Cisco Router, Nexus, XR, Cisco Meraki, Panorama NG, FortiManager, Azure, AWS, Arista CVP and EOS

This API verifies connection to the provided device, before adding a device.
This is done by:
  Cisco - Connect with SSH and see that the credentials are correct.
  Check Point (Excluding Smart 1 Cloud) - Test OPSEC connectivity. For R80 devices, this also verifies the connectivity for API login.
  Panorama - Login through the API to check connectivity.
  Fortimanager - Login through the API to check connectivity.
  Arista - The SSL certificate is retrieved.
If the connection test fails the device will not be added.
The connection is not verified for Smart 1 Cloud, Cisco Router,Nexus and XR.
Only "live" device with correct credentials can be added using this API.
For Cisco Router, Nexus and XR there are 2 usage examples : one only with mandatory fields (device name example : Router-Cisco-mandatory-fields) and one with both mandatory and optional fields ( device name example : Router-Cisco-all-fields).
Only for Cisco Router, the 'router_operating_system' field can take the following values: IOS, IOS-XE and IOS-XE-SDWAN. The latter corresponds to Cisco Viptela cEdge routers.

When adding a new AWS or Azure device and the field use_cloud_organization is set to true, than the cloud_organization and account_id or subscription _id fields become mandatory.
The value for cloud_organization must match the name defined in the Cloud Organization settings within TOS.
Only users with Super Admin privileges are authorized to use this setting.
When adding a new Azure subscription using the API, the usage analysis feature will be turned off by default, unless you explicitly specify "collect_rule_usage_traffic_logs" : true and also "collect_object_usage_traffic_logs" : true and also "rule_optimizer_usage" : true. See Azure example body - "azure_device"

Use this API to edit the settings of a management device, including changing multiple parameters in the same call.
The API body should include a JSON file with the mandatory parameters for the operation.
String parameters contain only lower and upper case letters, digits, space, dot, backward slash, forward slash, underscore or one of the following signs: #@:;=!^().
The following devices can be edited: Check Point R80+,Check Point Smart-1, Cisco ASA, Cisco Meraki, Panorama NG, Fortinet FortiManager, Microsoft Azure, Juniper MX and SRX, Amazon AWS, Arista CVP and EOS.
Microsoft Azure (we currently support editing vnet and management device).
Note: This API cannot be used to edit SecureTrack Domains and SecureTrack Servers.
This API verifies connection to the provided device, before updating a device. For Juniper MX only the username and password update is supported. No validation is done for these fields.
For Juniper SRX only the username, password and vault update is supported. Only the vault name is validated.
For the supported devices the validation is done by:
  Cisco - Connect with SSH and see that the credentials are correct.
  Check Point - Test OPSEC connectivity and verifies the connectivity for API login.
  Panorama - Login through the API to check connectivity.
  Fortimanager - Login through the API to check connectivity.
  Arista - The SSL certificate is retrieved.
If the connection test fails the device will not be updated.
Only "live" device with correct credentials can be updated using this API.
Only for Cisco Router, the 'router_operating_system' field can take the following values: IOS, IOS-XE and IOS-XE-SDWAN. The latter corresponds to Cisco Viptela cEdge routers.
When editing an AWS or Azure device and the field use_cloud_organization is set to true, then the field cloud_organization becomes mandatory. Additionally, the account_id or subscription_id fields can no longer be used.

Use this API to import more managed devices to SecureTrack.
The following devices can be added: Device Groups (for Panorama), Administrative Domains (for FortiManager), Account (for Meraki). Currently supported types: Panorama, FortiManager, Cisco ASA, Microsoft Azure, Cisco Meraki, Amazon AWS, Arista Eos.
Please see examples below per device id: device id 1 for Panorama, device 2 for FortiManager, device id 3 for Cisco ASA, device id 4 for Azure, device id 5 for Meraki, device id 6 for Amazon, device id 7 for Arista Eos.

operations.summary: Get specific task results of bulk operations on devices
operations.notes:
Use this API to track in-progress actions involving monitored devices. A list of devices and their status is displayed.
The API supports actions initiated by:
- /securetrack/api/devices/bulk - in the case of failure, the relevant device is added to the "failed" list with the status element containing the description. Example: "No connection to device. "
- /securetrack/api/devices/bulk/update_topology_data

responseMessages.message: The task with the given UID was not found

operations.notes: Returns textual configuration for the specified revision Id.

Returns the list of devices that are configured in SecureTrack, including the vendor, model, name, domain and device ID.
The results can be sorted by ip, name, vendor and model.
For more information on supported devices, supported models and virtual types please refer to 'Device and Revision Resources' section in the Tufin Knowledge Center .

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort ascending or descending allowable types are: asc or desc

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Starting page for query allowable type is numeric

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of pages for query starting from starting page allowable type in numeric

parameters.name: show_os_version
parameters.description: Show os version as apart of the response

parameters.name: show_license
parameters.description: Show license information for each device

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Device status, allowable values are: started, stopped, connected, disabled or error

operations.notes:
You can choose to select the domain either by name or id using the domain_id or domain_name tags.
Device name should contain only Lower and upper case letters, digits, space, dot, backward slash, forward slash, underscore or one of the following signs: #@:;=!^()

Returns the vendor, model, name, domain and ID for the specified device.
For more information on supported devices, supported models and virtual types please refer to 'Device and Revision Resources' section in the Tufin Knowledge Center .

type.description: The license category or feature type (4 options are available: SecureTrack, SecureChange, Provisioning, OS Monitoring)

expiration.description: ISO date of the license expiration date (last day when this license is valid)

status.description: License status, e.g., licensed, expired, plug&play, unlicensed, etc.

sku.description: The actual SKU name used by this device

OS_Version.description: The OS version of the firewall

context_name.description: The context name of the device

virtual_type.description: The virtual type of the device

parent_id.description: The parent ID of the device

installed_policy.description: Installed policy currently active on the device

domain_id.description: The domain identifier of the device

domain_name.description: The domain name of the device

domain_id.description: The domain identifier of the device

domain_name.description: The domain name of the device

display_name.description: required for POST, optional for PATCH

ip_address.description: required for POST, optional for PATCH
